C[Ssays: Well, Listening to joseki in these little environs is probably risky, but each move felt right.
Ssays: However, I have planned my departure from joseki if you take it down the standard path. For better or for worse.
C[Ssays: and I didn't get there.
C[rpchuang: Hard to say joseki, because standard joseki take into account a 19x19 board.
rpchuang: mostly trade-off between influence versus territory, whereas in 13x13 board, the trade offs at not as clear cut.
C[rpchuang: Maybe more biased toward territory in 13x13
Ssays: Think D8 would have been better?
rpchuang: Not really. Should probably connect at c8
C[Ssays: How would you feel about a resign from me?
Ssays: I get intimidated by a player's rank and do riskier things.
C[rpchuang: No big deal. Up to you if you feel you can learn more from game, please continue. If not then should resign.
rpchuang: As long as you are having fun!
rpchuang: You know that we are on Chinese counting?

Tarot: +I'm not the best at counting but I believe this is easily black's game [why do we have the wrong color in these tournaments, it astounds me\] unless white could later perform a succesful invasion of the NE corner/northside.
Tarot: Again, thank you kindly, I feel I learned quite a bit during this match.

C[topazg: Huh? I didn't play a move there :S
topazg: Apparently double click submits :P Let's see how it pans out.
C[Dragon Pie: Hello.  Have a fun game
C[Dragon Pie: Hello.  Have a fun game
C[Dragon Pie: I feel intimidated by three stones.  Four or more, I always feel like there's no chance, but somehow I win them often enough anyway.  Weird.
Dragon Pie: My other game on here, I went with the Magicwand style that I've seen him play in four or five stone handicap games.  This time, I go with my regular play.
C[Dragon Pie: Now, I get to see if he tenuki's, I can play some moves that a player a couple of stones weaker might find tricky to handle.  We'll see how it goes.  Fingers crossed.

C[Dragon Pie: This can go a few different ways.  Often him getting territory and me getting influence.  It's intimidating to have to catch up if he has more cash, but influence can sure help the cause.

C[Dragon Pie: It's also possible to attach and create a ko on the other side.  Or at least, I think it is when black responds at c6 instead of d6.  After this, the corner is still open and the prospect of me undercutting him on the left means that it doesn't make a ton of sense to close it.

C[Dragon Pie: His move makes some sense in this shape.  It doesn't prevent white from linking up his stone underneath later, so it leaves some aji in his position while giving me a bit more influence.  I can't say that it's a grievous mistake or anything.  I think considering the aji it leaves behind and the close proximity of his shimari, I do gain more from these exchanges.

C[Dragon Pie: This is a place that he'd like to play.  It gives me much better shape and threatens to cut his two stones and revive my stone.  He should respond and then I can play on the right to pressure his corner group.
C[Dragon Pie: I get to pressure his group now.  Let's see how he responds.

C[Dragon Pie: My opponents' last move was very passive.  Actually, his move before that wasn't very good either.  I thought he was just trying to avoid being sealed in, but it looks like he was unsure of the live of the group.  There's too much corner space for me to kill him not to mention the stone that he's caught.  I'm glad to get a free move at O8 and now to get sente out of the deal.  His last move was worth a couple of points, but not nearly big enough at this stage of the game.

C[Dragon Pie: My stones are a bit thin.  It's up to him to expose that thinness.  Often the best way is the patient way when it comes to exposing bad aji.  Exploiting it right away can help white make a decision.  He he cuts through my stones now, he could find himself with a heavy, weak group.  If he plays away, I will be burdened by it for the whole game.
C[Dragon Pie: He's got a big moyo on the left.  But now this is getting interesting.  Let's see how he responds.

C[Dragon Pie: I was going to just play L17, but then I realized that would encourage black to extend here which would be less than ideal for white, so I took this point to prevent black from getting it, but looking back, I realize it to be a mistake as black can hane here and I'd want to defend my cutting point, but I don't have time to if I want to murder black(which I do)
C[Dragon Pie: I guess he wanted to erase his aji.  Still, I'm happy that my bad move didn't come to fruition.

C[Dragon Pie: I'm pretty sure I can deal with this.  I don't see any move that threatens to do anything particularly harmful to my position, though maybe he can try some funny business with his stone at Q11.
C[Dragon Pie: This is why I didn't feel the need to protect the cut earlier and went straight into 3-3.

C[Dragon Pie: I don't want to get into some sort of capture race with the Q10 stone, so I take care of my own and let him try to solve the tough life and death problem in front of him.  If he lives I get sente and a move around H10 looks like it builds for me and reduces him.  Still, high handi games like this are tough.
C[Dragon Pie: I see some tricky plays in his position, but no easy way to live.

Dragon Pie: R18, S19 R19  looks tricky.  after T16.  if he squeezes at R13, R12,S12.  But if I just take and fill, I end up with three liberties which is just enough to deal with his descent there, so I can capture those two stones which means that I am ok to cut at Q18 in that event after he double hane's and after I escape, it won't remain a ko because I won't be in atari and I can just play at P19 to kill his stones.  Close to scary though.
C[Dragon Pie: I hadn't really considered this move.  :)
C[Dragon Pie: I guess he's trying to decrease the ko aji there?
Dragon Pie: Not sure what meaning was behind his last move.
C[Dragon Pie: Well I can kill his group right now which would be worth about 55 points, I think.

Dragon Pie: H10 looks absolutely huge too and it has follow ups worth some major points, so I'm going to play there first before my opponent gets a chance.  It's on the border of our moyos.  If he lives in gote, I'll try to pull off something with sente.  Maybe I'll invade his left.

Dragon Pie: Oh, and I meant that the top right looks like 45 points.  Which is huge, but I really do think that H10 is worth a similar amount and that getting to play again after he follows up with living will make it the bigger move.

C[Dragon Pie: I'd be happy to have my territory solidified here and to take sente to go back and kill his group. His move was playing into the hane at the head of two stones. Sometimes it's an ok play, but typically, it's bad shape  Because it's short on liberties.
Dragon Pie: Now, THIS move surprised me, but I suppose it shouldn't have because he's pretty strong in the area, but I think I can fight it.
C[Dragon Pie: Definitely Kudos to him for fighting spirit.

C[Mef: If W seals B off in sente, it's close but I think B has an edge
Mef: B is alive though, F14 - H18 - E15  makes E17 and E16 miai

Mef: White G2 is terribly annnoying (G2 - G3 - H4  leaves J2 and F2 miai for white)
C[Mef: This works too, B should live, but now W taking away the point at E14 is sente
Mef: F14-E14-H19-E16-D17 -E17 - C19 - E19.....I guess technically a ko?  but B has many local threats W won't win it
Mef: B would capture first and have H18, J19, D8, C15, B13  and possibly threats to link up to the bottom?
C[Mef: Risky - this is a lot of points to give up for sure life, but B will have sente to play R15
C[Mef: W has about 87 solid points now?  Along with 6.5 komi, and honestly he should make a handful more on bottom...  That's how much B needs on the right
Mef: that's assuming nothing for white lives right of the Q line
Mef: 15 more if you assume nothing between O8 and O16 lives for white
Mef: so if B R15, even a move like N12 makes it really tough for B to catch up
C[Mef: Oh, B wants to live or connect with those stones...tall order
